

WIF2003  ·  Web Programming

**Lets Go / EcoPlanner**

**Unit, Functional, Integration & Database Testing Document**

Prepared by

**OCC1 GROUP2**

Faculty of Computer Science & Information Technology

Version 1.0  ·  2026

# **1\. Introduction**

This document presents the Unit Testing, Functional Testing, Integration Testing and Database Testing plan and expected results for the Lets Go / EcoPlanner web application. The format follows the WIF2003 reference testing document: each major test is documented with a test ID, module, description, precondition, test data or route, test code where applicable, and an expected-result table.

## **1.1 Application Under Test**

Application Name: Lets Go / EcoPlanner \- Eco-Friendly Travel Planner

Technology Stack: Node.js, Express.js 5.x, MongoDB, Mongoose ODM, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, Bootstrap 5

Entry Point: server.js

Port: 3000

Database: MongoDB / MongoDB Atlas

Main Modules: Authentication, User Profile, Explore Destinations, Favourites, Trip Planner, Carbon Calculation

## **1.2 Testing Objectives**

* Validate isolated helper logic such as password validation, email normalisation, carbon calculation and destination price filtering.  
* Verify visible browser behaviour for registration, login, profile management, destination search, favourites and trip planning.  
* Confirm Express routes, authentication middleware, model methods and API responses integrate correctly.  
* Check MongoDB schema rules, required fields, enum constraints, password hashing, ownership fields and CRUD consistency.  
* Provide clear pass/fail summary tables and evidence requirements for final report submission.

## **1.3 Testing Tools & Setup**

| Tool | Version / Source | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Node.js | 18+ | Runtime environment and native fetch support |
| node:test | Built-in | JavaScript unit and integration test runner |
| node:assert/strict | Built-in | Strict assertion library |
| Express.js | 5.2.1 | Backend route handling under test |
| Mongoose | 9.3.3 | Schema validation and model-layer database testing |
| MongoDB / Atlas | Cloud or local | Database CRUD and evidence verification |
| Chrome / Edge | Latest | Functional browser testing |
| Postman / Thunder Client | Latest | API and integration testing |

## **1.4 Setup Commands**

Run the following commands before executing the test suite:

| npm install\# create .env manually with MONGO\_URI, PORT and JWT\_SECRETnpm run seed    \# optional, if seed data is needednpm startnpm test |
| :---- |

# **2\. Unit Testing**

Unit tests validate individual, isolated parts of the application \- helper functions, calculation logic and schema-level rules \- independent of a full browser flow.

## **UT-01 · Password Too Short Validation**

Validates that passwords shorter than the configured minimum length are rejected before registration.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-01 |
| Module | routes/auth.js \- validatePasswordLength() |
| Description | Password shorter than 8 characters must return a validation message. |
| Precondition | The auth helper is exported from routes/auth.js. |
| Test Data | "abc123" |

**Test Code**

| const { helpers } \= require("../routes/auth");test("short password is rejected", () \=\> {  assert.match(helpers.validatePasswordLength("abc123"), /at least 8 characters/);}); |
| :---- |
| ![][image1] |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Call validatePasswordLength("abc123") | Returns message containing "at least 8 characters" | **PASS** |
| 2 | Assert returned value is not empty | Validation fails as expected | **PASS** |

## **UT-02 · Valid Password Length**

Validates that acceptable password length returns no validation error.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-02 |
| Module | routes/auth.js \- validatePasswordLength() |
| Description | Password with 8 or more characters must pass length validation. |
| Precondition | The auth helper is importable. |
| Test Data | "password123" |

**Test Code**

| const { helpers } \= require("../routes/auth");test("valid password length passes", () \=\> {  assert.equal(helpers.validatePasswordLength("password123"), "");});  ![][image2] |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Call validatePasswordLength("password123") | Returns empty string | **PASS** |
| 2 | Compare result with empty string | Validation passes | **PASS** |

## **UT-03 · Email Normalisation**

Validates that user email values are trimmed and converted to lowercase before ownership checks.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-03 |
| Module | routes/trips.js \- normalizeEmail() |
| Description | Email should be cleaned before comparing route parameter email with token email. |
| Precondition | The trip helper is exported from routes/trips.js. |
| Test Data | "  Demo@EcoPlanner.COM  " |

**Test Code**

| const { helpers } \= require("../routes/trips");test("email is normalised", () \=\> {  assert.equal(helpers.normalizeEmail("  Demo@EcoPlanner.COM  "), "demo@ecoplanner.com");}); ![][image3] |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Call normalizeEmail with spaces and uppercase letters | Returns demo@ecoplanner.com | **PASS** |

## **UT-04 · Carbon Calculation for Walking Route**

Validates the route carbon helper using two stops and walking transport mode.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-04 |
| Module | routes/trips.js \- calculateRouteCarbon() |
| Description | Walking route should produce a positive distance and zero carbon footprint. |
| Precondition | Two stops have valid latitude and longitude. |
| Test Data | KLCC to Merdeka Square sample coordinates, transportMode \= walking |

**Test Code**

| const result \= helpers.calculateRouteCarbon(stops, "walking");assert.equal(result.transportMode, "walking");assert.equal(result.carbonFootprintKg, 0);assert.ok(result.distanceKm \> 0); ![][image4] |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Prepare two valid coordinate stops | Stops array is valid | **PASS** |
| 2 | Call calculateRouteCarbon(stops, "walking") | carbonFootprintKg is 0 and distanceKm \> 0 | **PASS** |

## **UT-05 · Unknown Transport Mode Fallback**

Validates that an unsupported transport mode falls back to the petrol car baseline.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-05 |
| Module | routes/trips.js \- calculateRouteCarbon() |
| Description | Unknown transport mode should not crash the calculation. |
| Precondition | Two valid stops are prepared. |
| Test Data | transportMode \= spaceship |

**Test Code**

| const result \= helpers.calculateRouteCarbon(stops, "spaceship");assert.equal(result.transportMode, "car\_petrol");assert.ok(result.carbonFootprintKg \>= 0); ![][image5] |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Call calculateRouteCarbon with unknown mode | Function does not crash | **PASS** |
| 2 | Assert transportMode is car\_petrol | Fallback mode is applied | **PASS** |

## **UT-06 · Destination Price Filter**

Validates that destination filtering keeps only records inside the selected price range.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | UT-06 |
| Module | routes/destinations.js \- filterByPrice() |
| Description | Only destinations within min and max price should remain. |
| Precondition | Mock destinations with priceLabel values are prepared. |
| Test Data | RM20, RM80, RM200 with min=50 and max=150 |

**Test Code**

| const { helpers } \= require("../routes/destinations");const filtered \= helpers.filterByPrice(destinations, 50, 150);assert.equal(filtered.length, 1);assert.equal(filtered\[0\].priceLabel, "RM80"); ![][image6] |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Prepare RM20, RM80 and RM200 mock records | Mock data ready | **PASS** |
| 2 | Filter with min=50 and max=150 | Only RM80 remains | **PASS** |

# **3\. Functional Testing**

Functional tests validate end-to-end browser behaviour from the user perspective. The tester interacts with the deployed page, records the visible result, and captures browser screenshots as evidence.

## **FT-01 · User Registration Page**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for user registration page.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-01 |
| Route / Module | POST /api/auth/register through register UI |
| Description | Register a new user with valid name, email and password. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Register a new user with valid name, email and password. | Successful registration message is shown and user can proceed to login. | **PASS** |

## **FT-02 · User Login Page**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for user login page.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-02 |
| Route / Module | POST /api/auth/login through login UI |
| Description | Login using registered email and correct password. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Login using registered email and correct password. | User is redirected to dashboard/profile and session token is stored. | **PASS** |

## **FT-03 · Profile Retrieval**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for profile retrieval.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-03 |
| Route / Module | GET /api/users/profile/:email through profile page |
| Description | Open profile after login. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Open profile after login. | Name, email, city, budget, interests and carbon information display correctly. | **PASS** |

## **FT-04 · Profile Update**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for profile update.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-04 |
| Route / Module | PUT /api/users/:email through profile form |
| Description | Edit city, budget, interests and notification preferences. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Edit city, budget, interests and notification preferences. | Updated values remain visible after save and refresh. | **PASS** |

**FT-05 · Explore Destination Listing**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for explore destination listing.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-05 |
| Route / Module | GET /api/destinations through Explore page |
| Description | Open Explore page and wait for cards to load. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Open Explore page and wait for cards to load. | Destination cards are loaded dynamically from API response. | **PASS** |

## **FT-06 · Search and Filter Destinations**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for search and filter destinations.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-06 |
| Route / Module | GET /api/destinations with query parameters |
| Description | Search by destination/city/category and apply budget or eco filters. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Search by destination/city/category and apply budget or eco filters. | Only matching destination cards are shown. | **PASS** |

## **FT-07 · Favourite Destination Toggle**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for favourite destination toggle.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-07 |
| Route / Module | PUT /api/users/:email/favorites |
| Description | Click favourite icon on a destination card. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Click favourite icon on a destination card. | Favourite state changes without full page reload and remains synced. | **PASS** |

## **FT-08 · Itinerary Planner Create Trip**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for itinerary planner create trip.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-08 |
| Route / Module | POST /api/trips through planner UI |
| Description | Add destinations and create an itinerary in planner. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Add destinations and create an itinerary in planner. | Trip appears in planner timeline with selected activities. | **PASS** |

## **FT-09 · Carbon Calculation UI**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for carbon calculation ui.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-09 |
| Route / Module | POST /api/trips/calculate-carbon through planner/carbon feature |
| Description | Submit route stops and transport mode. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Submit route stops and transport mode. | Distance, carbon footprint, baseline and saved carbon display correctly. | **PASS** |

## **FT-10 · Invalid Route Handling**

Verifies the browser-visible behaviour for invalid route handling.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | FT-10 |
| Route / Module | GET /unknown-page |
| Description | Visit an undefined page. |
| Precondition | Server is running at http://localhost:3000 and relevant test account/data are available. |
| Expected Status | Successful request or correct error handling, depending on test case. |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Visit an undefined page. | System shows Not found / 404 response without crashing. | **PASS** |

# **4\. Integration Testing**

Integration tests verify that the front-end request, Express route, authentication middleware, Mongoose model operation, cache update and JSON response work together correctly. API stubbing can be used when a live MongoDB instance is not required.

## **IT-01 · Registration API Integration**

Validates the register flow from request body to User model creation.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | IT-01 |
| Route | POST /api/auth/register |
| Description | Request with valid user data should pass required-field, email-format and duplicate-email checks before creating the user. |
| Precondition | Auth route is mounted and User.findOne/User.save are available or stubbed. |
| Test Data | name, email, password |

**Test Code**

| const res \= await fetch(\`${baseUrl}/api/auth/register\`, {  method: "POST",  headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },  body: JSON.stringify({ name, email, password })});assert.equal(res.status, 201); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Submit valid registration request | HTTP 201 Created is returned | **PASS** |
| 2 | Check duplicate email branch first | Existing email is rejected with 400 | **PASS** |

## **IT-02 · Login and Token Integration**

Validates the login flow from credentials to password comparison and token response.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | IT-02 |
| Route | POST /api/auth/login |
| Description | Correct credentials should return success response with signed token. |
| Precondition | User record exists and comparePassword() is available or stubbed. |
| Test Data | registered email and correct password |

**Test Code**

| const res \= await fetch(\`${baseUrl}/api/auth/login\`, requestOptions);const body \= await res.json();assert.equal(res.status, 200);assert.equal(body.success, true);assert.ok(body.token); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Login with correct credentials | HTTP 200 and token returned | **PASS** |
| 2 | Login with incorrect password | HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned | **PASS** |

## **IT-03 · Protected Profile Integration**

Validates middleware and profile retrieval working together.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | IT-03 |
| Route | GET /api/users/profile/:email |
| Description | requireAuth and requireSelfEmail must allow the owner and block other emails. |
| Precondition | Valid Bearer token is prepared. |
| Test Data | demo@ecoplanner.com |

**Test Code**

| const res \= await fetch(\`${baseUrl}/api/users/profile/demo@ecoplanner.com\`, {  headers: { Authorization: \`Bearer ${token}\` }});assert.equal(res.status, 200); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Request own profile with valid token | Profile JSON is returned | **PASS** |
| 2 | Request another user profile | Access is blocked with 403 | **PASS** |

## **IT-04 · Destination API with Stubbed Model**

Validates destination route response without requiring a live MongoDB connection.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | IT-04 |
| Route | GET /api/destinations |
| Description | Destination.find is temporarily stubbed to return sample records. |
| Precondition | Express dynamic server starts on a random port. |
| Test Data | Sample destination array |

**Test Code**

| const response \= await fetch(\`${baseUrl}/api/destinations\`);const payload \= await response.json();assert.equal(response.status, 200);assert.equal(payload.success, true);assert.equal(payload.data\[0\].name, "Eco Stay"); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Stub Destination.find to return sample array | Model method is restored after test | **PASS** |
| 2 | GET /api/destinations | Response status is 200 and success is true | **PASS** |
| 3 | Read payload.data\[0\].name | Returned name matches sample destination | **PASS** |

## **IT-05 · Trip Ownership Integration**

Validates that trip create/update/delete operations are scoped to the authenticated user.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | IT-05 |
| Route | POST/PUT/DELETE /api/trips |
| Description | Trip userEmail must come from token and cross-account updates/deletes must be blocked. |
| Precondition | Valid token and trip document are prepared. |
| Test Data | Owned trip and another user trip |

**Test Code**

| assert.equal(createdTrip.userEmail, tokenEmail);assert.equal(deleteOtherUserTripResponse.status, 403); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Create trip while authenticated | Trip stores userEmail from token | **PASS** |
| 2 | Update or delete owned trip | Operation succeeds | **PASS** |
| 3 | Update or delete another user trip | Operation returns 403 | **PASS** |

# **5\. Database Testing**

Database tests validate MongoDB and Mongoose behaviour, including schema constraints, required fields, enum validation, password hashing, unique email handling, CRUD persistence and data consistency after update/delete actions.

## **DB-01 · Valid User Insert and Password Hashing**

Verifies that a valid user document is created and password is stored as a hash.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-01 |
| Module | models/User.js |
| Description | Valid user should satisfy schema rules and pre-save hook should hash password. |
| Precondition | MongoDB connection or Mongoose validation context is available. |
| Test Data | name, email, password |

**Test Code**

| const user \= new User({ name: "Demo User", email: "demo@test.com", password: "password123" });const error \= user.validateSync();assert.equal(error, undefined); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Create valid user document | Document is valid | **PASS** |
| 2 | Save user and inspect password | Stored password is bcrypt hash, not plain text | **PASS** |
| 3 | Convert to JSON | Password is not exposed | **PASS** |

## **DB-02 · Duplicate User Email Rejection**

Verifies that the database/user logic prevents duplicate account email records.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-02 |
| Module | models/User.js \+ routes/auth.js |
| Description | Second registration with an existing email should be rejected. |
| Precondition | One user with the test email already exists. |
| Test Data | existing demo@test.com email |

**Test Code**

| const existingUser \= await User.findOne({ email });assert.ok(existingUser);// Route returns 400: Email is already registered. |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Register using existing email | API returns duplicate email error | **PASS** |
| 2 | Query users collection for the email | Only one document exists | **PASS** |

## **DB-03 · Invalid User Email Format**

Verifies that invalid email format is rejected by model and route validation.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-03 |
| Module | models/User.js |
| Description | Invalid email should trigger validation error and no user document should be inserted. |
| Precondition | User schema email match rule is available. |
| Test Data | user@@test |

**Test Code**

| const user \= new User({ name: "Demo User", email: "user@@test", password: "password123" });const error \= user.validateSync();assert.ok(error.errors.email); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Submit invalid email user@@test | Validation fails | **PASS** |
| 2 | Search users collection | No invalid document exists | **PASS** |

## **DB-04 · Budget Normalisation**

Verifies that legacy budget values are normalised before persistence.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-04 |
| Module | models/User.js \- normalizeBudget() |
| Description | legacy value budget maps to low and luxury maps to high. |
| Precondition | User.normalizeBudget static method is available. |
| Test Data | budget, luxury |

**Test Code**

| assert.equal(User.normalizeBudget("budget"), "low");assert.equal(User.normalizeBudget("luxury"), "high"); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Update profile with budget | Stored value is low | **PASS** |
| 2 | Update profile with luxury | Stored value is high | **PASS** |

## **DB-05 · Destination Schema Constraints**

Verifies destination required fields, category enum and ecoScore range.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-05 |
| Module | models/Destination.js |
| Description | Invalid category and ecoScore above 10 should trigger validation errors. |
| Precondition | Destination model is importable. |
| Test Data | category \= invalid-category, ecoScore \= 11 |

**Test Code**

| const destination \= new Destination({  id: 1, name: "Eco Stay", location: "KL", category: "invalid-category",  ecoScore: 11, imageUrl: "x.jpg", description: "test"});const error \= destination.validateSync();assert.ok(error.errors.category);assert.ok(error.errors.ecoScore); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Create invalid destination object | category validation error appears | **PASS** |
| 2 | Set ecoScore above 10 | ecoScore validation error appears | **PASS** |

## **DB-06 · Destination Retrieval and Filtering**

Verifies that destination documents are retrieved and filtered consistently.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-06 |
| Module | routes/destinations.js \+ models/Destination.js |
| Description | GET /api/destinations should return records that match search/category/eco/price filters. |
| Precondition | Destination collection contains seeded data. |
| Test Data | search/category/minEco/minPrice/maxPrice |

**Test Code**

| GET /api/destinations?category=hotel\&minEco=8\&minPrice=50\&maxPrice=150 |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Call GET /api/destinations with filters | Returned records match query and price criteria | **PASS** |
| 2 | Compare with Explore UI | UI displays same filtered records | **PASS** |

## **DB-07 · Valid Trip Creation and Ownership**

Verifies that a trip document is created with correct userEmail ownership.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-07 |
| Module | models/Trip.js \+ routes/trips.js |
| Description | Trip document should store required fields and userEmail from authenticated token. |
| Precondition | User is authenticated and valid trip data is prepared. |
| Test Data | name, city, start, end, days |

**Test Code**

| const trip \= await Trip.create({ ...tripData, userEmail: tokenEmail });assert.equal(trip.userEmail, tokenEmail); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Create trip while authenticated | Trip document exists in trips collection | **PASS** |
| 2 | Inspect userEmail field | userEmail matches token email | **PASS** |

## **DB-08 · Invalid Trip Date Range Rejection**

Verifies that trip end date before start date is rejected.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-08 |
| Module | models/Trip.js |
| Description | The pre-validate hook should invalidate chronological errors. |
| Precondition | Trip model is importable. |
| Test Data | start \= 2026-06-20, end \= 2026-06-10 |

**Test Code**

| const trip \= new Trip({ userEmail, name: "Test Trip", start, end });const error \= trip.validateSync();assert.ok(error.errors.end); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Create trip with end date before start date | Trip validation rejects end date | **PASS** |
| 2 | Search trips collection for invalid trip | No invalid document is saved | **PASS** |

## **DB-09 · Trip Update/Delete Consistency**

Verifies update and delete operations maintain database consistency.

| Field | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Test ID | DB-09 |
| Module | routes/trips.js \+ models/Trip.js |
| Description | Owned trip update should change the same document; delete should remove it cleanly. |
| Precondition | An owned trip document exists. |
| Test Data | existing trip ID |

**Test Code**

| await Trip.findByIdAndUpdate(id, updatedData, { new: true, runValidators: true });await Trip.findByIdAndDelete(id);assert.equal(await Trip.findById(id), null); |
| :---- |

**Expected Results**

| \# | Test Case | Expected | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Update existing owned trip | Same document ID has updated values | **PASS** |
| 2 | Delete the trip | Deleted ID no longer exists | **PASS** |
| 3 | Reload planner page | Deleted trip is no longer displayed | **PASS** |

# **6\. Test Summary & Results**

The table below summarises all test cases covered in this document across Unit Testing, Functional Testing, Integration Testing and Database Testing categories.

## **6.1 Overall Test Results**

| Test ID | Type | Description | Category | Status |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| UT-01 | Unit | Password too short validation | Validation | **PASS** |
| UT-02 | Unit | Valid password length | Validation | **PASS** |
| UT-03 | Unit | Email normalisation | Data Handling | **PASS** |
| UT-04 | Unit | Walking carbon calculation | Calculation | **PASS** |
| UT-05 | Unit | Unknown transport fallback | Boundary | **PASS** |
| UT-06 | Unit | Destination price filter | Filtering | **PASS** |
| FT-01 | Functional | User registration page | Browser/UI | **PASS** |
| FT-02 | Functional | User login page | Browser/UI | **PASS** |
| FT-03 | Functional | Profile retrieval | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-04 | Functional | Profile update | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-05 | Functional | Explore listing | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-06 | Functional | Search and filter destinations | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-07 | Functional | Favourite destination toggle | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-08 | Functional | Itinerary planner create trip | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-09 | Functional | Carbon calculation UI | Browser/API | **PASS** |
| FT-10 | Functional | Invalid route handling | Error Handling | **PASS** |
| IT-01 | Integration | Registration API integration | API/Model | **PASS** |
| IT-02 | Integration | Login and token integration | API/Middleware | **PASS** |
| IT-03 | Integration | Protected profile integration | Middleware/API | **PASS** |
| IT-04 | Integration | Destination API with stubbed model | API/Model | **PASS** |
| IT-05 | Integration | Trip ownership integration | Security/Data | **PASS** |
| DB-01 | Database | Valid user insert and password hashing | Schema/Security | **PASS** |
| DB-02 | Database | Duplicate user email rejection | Uniqueness | **PASS** |
| DB-03 | Database | Invalid user email format | Validation | **PASS** |
| DB-04 | Database | Budget normalisation | Validation | **PASS** |
| DB-05 | Database | Destination schema constraints | Validation | **PASS** |
| DB-06 | Database | Destination retrieval and filtering | Query | **PASS** |
| DB-07 | Database | Valid trip creation and ownership | CRUD/Ownership | **PASS** |
| DB-08 | Database | Invalid trip date range rejection | Validation | **PASS** |
| DB-09 | Database | Trip update/delete consistency | CRUD | **PASS** |

## **6.2 Test Coverage Summary**

| Category | Total Tests | Passed | Failed |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Unit Tests | 6 | 6 | 0 |
| Functional Tests | 10 | 10 | 0 |
| Integration Tests | 5 | 5 | 0 |
| Database Tests | 9 | 9 | 0 |
| **TOTAL** | **30** | **30** | **0** |

##

| Evidence No. | Evidence Required | Status |

| FT-E09 | Itinerary Planner Create Trip | ![][image22]  ![][image23] |

| FT-E11 | Invalid Route Handling | ![][image25] |
| IT-E01 | Registration API Integration | ![][image26] |
| IT-E02 | Login Token Retrieval | ![][image27] |
| IT-E03 | Protected Profile Auth  | ![][image28] |
| IT-E04 | Explore Destination API Flow  | ![][image29] |
| IT-E05 | Itinerary Trip CRUD API Flow | ![][image30]  |
| IT-E06 | Carbon Calculation API | ![][image31]  ![][image32] |
| IT-E07 | Terminal output showing npm test passed | ![][image33] |
| IT-E08 | Source Code of Integration Test | ![][image34]![][image35]![][image36]![][image37]![][image38]![][image39] |
| DB-E01 | Password Hashing Verification | ![][image40] |
| DB-E02 | Email Uniqueness Validation  | ![][image41] |
| DB-E03 | Invalid User Rejection | ![][image42] |
| DB-E04 | Profile Update Persistence | **![][image43]** **After (Updated)** **![][image44]** |
| DB-E05 | Destination Collection Consistency | **![][image45]![][image46]** Before  vs  After (array in days changed) Trip Day Itinerary Update *Before  and After* |
| DB-E06 | Trip Creation & Email Ownership | **![][image46]** |
| DB-E07 | Trip Date Validation Rejection | ![][image47]  |
| DB-E08 | Trip Deletion Consistency | **![][image48]** **Mongoose DB Trips model Before Click Delete Trip**  **![][image49] Mongoose DB Trips model After Click Delete Trip** No more trips from this user are displayed  |
| DB-E09 | Terminal output showing npm test passed | **![][image50]** |

## **6.4 Export Setup for Testing**

To make route helpers and the Express app accessible to automated tests, keep exports at the bottom of route or server files where needed:

| module.exports \= app;module.exports.helpers \= { validatePasswordLength };module.exports.helpers \= { calculateRouteCarbon, normalizeEmail };module.exports.helpers \= { filterByPrice }; |
| :---- |

## **6.5 Running All Tests**

Execute the full test suite with:

| npm test |
| :---- |

Expected output:

| PASS  tests/models.test.jsPASS  tests/auth-route.test.jsPASS  tests/users-route.test.jsPASS  tests/trips-route.test.jsPASS  tests/destinations-route.test.jsPASS  tests/middleware.test.jsTests: 30 passed, 30 totalTest Suites: all passed |
| :---- |
